CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Feb-21-2017 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only.)
Off Calendar Jan-03-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
(Mini-minutes continued from previous entry)

Fact discovery cut-off date is March 30, 2017. Expert discovery cut-off date is April 14, 2017. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than February 3, 2017. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than January 12, 2017 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than January 12, 2017 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on February 22, 2017 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on April 17, 2017 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by April 13, 2017 at 2:00p.m. No opposition filed. Order signed. Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor, Reporter: Beth E. Grant (Independent Contractor), CSR # 10943 (Tel: 707-888-3689) (503/LOT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Dec-29-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Tiffany Viveros of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving parties Laura Gigi, et al.
Constance Fraenkel of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel
Jennifer Ways of PRINDLE, GOETZ, BARNES & REINHOLTZ LLP (415.788.8354), appearing for Defendant Chevron, USA

RULING: NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF.

Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(e). The October 23, 2017, 2017 trial date is advanced to April 17, 2017 at 11:15am in Department 503 with preference status. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is April 28, 2017. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 and the Informal Discovery Conference Order dated July 17, 2015, now included in Local Rule 20, shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is April 12, 2017. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than March 3, 2017. Responses to the demand are due no later than March 17, 2017. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by April 10, 2017 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on April 13, 2017. (Mini minutes continued, see next entry)
